title: 20 men, a 'sperm party,' and two days of sexual torment: The brutality of the 'libertine' rapes case
subhead: Over the course of a long weekend, one of Christophe B.'s former partners was forced to endure sexual assaults by numerous men at their home. Some of these assaults were broadcast live on the swinging website Wyylde.

Trump faces pressure to escalate in Iran after deaths of two US soldiers
subhead: The resumption of strikes and other acts of intimidation between Americans and Iranians on July 7 has undermined the protocol agreement signed in June. The US president, searching for a strategy, appears at a loss as the conflict drags on.

title: 'Can we continue to entrust global sport to organizations more concerned with their financial interests than with the public good?'
subhead: The excessive commercialization and blatant conflicts of interest that have characterized the 2026 World Cup should convince governments, athletes and other sports stakeholders to take back control, argue researchers Frédérique Reynertz and Pim Verschuuren.

title: BTS electrifies Stade de France, seven years after their last Paris concert
subhead: The world tour of the industry's most powerful K-pop group wrapped up its European leg in Saint-Denis on July 17 and July 18.

title: Inside the court of France's richest man, Bernard Arnault
subhead: 'Bernard Arnault's Empire' (1/6). The billionaire at the head of LVMH is as secretive as he is powerful. He relies on a devoted entourage in which a mix of fear and admiration prevails.
